author: y4rr
content:
Result: added memory categories, FLOPS estimation, and stack trace capture to ferrotorch-profiler. Schedule was already in place from earlier work.

**Memory categories** (event.rs + profiler.rs):
- New `MemoryCategory` enum: Activations, Parameters, OptimizerState, Gradients, Other (default)
- ProfileEvent gains `memory_category: Option<MemoryCategory>` field
- New `Profiler::record_memory_categorized(name, bytes, category)` API
- The existing `record_memory(name, bytes)` defaults to MemoryCategory::Other for back-compat
- New `ProfileReport::memory_by_category()` returns `Vec<(MemoryCategory, i64)>` sorted by absolute byte size (largest category first), with allocations/frees netted per category

**FLOPS estimation** (new flops.rs module):
- `flops::estimate(op_name, shapes) -> Option<u64>` returns dense compute cost for an op given its name and input shapes
- Counts MACs as 2 FLOPs (multiply + add), matching torch.profiler convention
- Coverage: elementwise binary (add/sub/mul/div), elementwise unary (neg/abs/sqrt/exp/log), activations (relu/sigmoid/tanh/gelu/silu/leaky_relu), softmax (5 * numel approximation), reductions (sum/mean/prod), pow, matmul/mm/bmm/linear (with batched broadcast), conv1d/2d/3d (assumes stride=1, padding='same' for spatial output size), norm layers (layer_norm/rms_norm/batch_norm/group_norm)
- ProfileEvent gains `flops: Option<u64>` field
- `Profiler::record` and the OpProfiler::record_op auto-instrumentation hook now compute and store FLOPS when record_shapes is on
- `ProfileReport::total_flops()` sums all event FLOPS
- `ProfileReport::flops_per_second()` divides total_flops by total_time_us, returns 0 when no time elapsed

**Stack traces** (event.rs + profiler.rs):
- ProfileEvent gains `stack_trace: Option<String>` field
- New `Profiler::maybe_capture_stack()` helper uses `std::backtrace::Backtrace::capture()` which is a no-op unless RUST_BACKTRACE env var is set, so the runtime cost when with_stack=true but the env is unset is just a few atomic loads
- The existing `with_stack: bool` config flag now actually does something — wired through every event-construction site (record, record_with_duration, record_memory*, push_gpu_event, OpProfiler::record_op)
- New `ProfileReport::has_stack_traces()` for quick checks

**Tests added (14 new in integration tests, 14 new in flops module unit tests):**

flops module (in flops.rs):
- elementwise_add_2d, elementwise_with_broadcast, unary_relu, softmax_approx, matmul_2d_2d, matmul_batched, matmul_dot_1d, matmul_inner_mismatch_returns_none, conv2d, conv1d, unknown_op_returns_none, no_shapes_returns_none, layer_norm_estimate, sum_reduction

Integration tests (in tests/profiler_tests.rs):
- memory_category_recorded_when_specified — verifies all 5 MemoryCategory variants land in the right field
- memory_by_category_aggregates_totals — sums per-category bytes including negative frees, sorted by absolute size
- record_memory_default_uses_other_category — back-compat for the original record_memory API
- flops_estimated_for_recorded_ops — verifies add/matmul/relu FLOPS values match the estimator
- flops_none_when_record_shapes_off — no shapes means no estimate
- total_flops_sums_event_flops — sum across multiple events
- flops_per_second_zero_when_no_time, _nonzero_with_durations — both edge cases of the per-second math
- auto_instrumented_op_gets_flops_estimate — verifies the OpProfiler hook also populates flops
- stack_trace_captured_when_with_stack_enabled, _none_when_disabled — verifies the with_stack flag actually controls capture
- memory_by_category_empty_when_no_memory_events — degenerate case

30 ferrotorch-profiler lib tests pass (was 16, +14). 33 integration tests pass (was 21, +12). `cargo build --workspace` clean with no new warnings.
